title: Johnson & Johnson — Immunology — briefing Q3-2025
html:
J&J Immunology Quarterly Briefing — Q3 2025

Quarter: July–September 2025 Earnings reported: J&J October 14 | AbbVie October 2025 | Novartis October 2025 | BMS October 2025 | UCB October 2025 | Lilly October 2025 | Amgen October 2025 Generated: 2026-04-27

Governing Thought

TREMFYA's 40% Q3 growth marks the beginning of GI-driven acceleration — but AbbVie's Skyrizi reached a $16B+ annual run rate the same quarter with 55% growth, outpacing TREMFYA in both scale and momentum. The IL-23 class in UC is crossing from 5% to 20%+ penetration at explosive speed, benefiting both brands — but AbbVie's head-to-head data (SEQUENCE in CD), earlier IBD launch, and Rinvoq JAK complement mean Skyrizi is capturing the larger share of the class expansion. Q3 is the quarter where TREMFYA proved GI works; it's also the quarter where AbbVie proved it's winning the GI race.

1. J&J Financial Signals

Metric Q3 2025 Commentary
J&J total sales $24.0B 6.8% reported growth; raised FY2025 outlook
TREMFYA growth +40.1% YoY Crohn's + UC driving acceleration
Immunology segment Declining Stelara erosion beginning to materialize at segment level
  • TREMFYA 40% growth in Q3 confirms the GI thesis — UC and CD approvals are generating real revenue
  • Stelara biosimilar competition materializing; J&J acknowledges accelerating erosion
  • J&J raised full-year 2025 outlook on strength of TREMFYA + oncology growth

What J&J management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. "TREMFYA will be a multi-billion dollar franchise" — management used the word "franchise," not "product," signaling TREMFYA is viewed internally as a portfolio-defining asset spanning derm, rheum, and GI. The multi-billion language sets a floor well above $5B.
  2. UC indication advancement highlighted specifically — J&J called out UC expansion as a key Q3 driver. GI is being positioned as the growth vector that differentiates TREMFYA from Skyrizi's earlier IBD launch.
  3. Revenue guidance raised on TREMFYA + oncology strength — the explicit pairing of TREMFYA with oncology as the two growth drivers signals TREMFYA's importance to J&J's overall portfolio narrative.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked about Skyrizi head-to-head positioning — the most important competitive question was raised directly: how does TREMFYA compete against Skyrizi's SEQUENCE data? J&J deflected to "differentiated IL-23 profile" rather than addressing the head-to-head data gap. This deflection signals J&J has no near-term plan to run a direct Skyrizi comparison.
  2. The deflection to "differentiated IL-23 profile" is the tell — when a company avoids a specific competitive comparison, it means they're vulnerable on that dimension. TREMFYA's lack of head-to-head vs. Skyrizi in CD remains the single most important competitive liability.
  3. No analyst probed ICOTYDE timeline — the oral IL-23 pipeline asset was not discussed, suggesting the Street is focused on TREMFYA's injectable franchise rather than J&J's oral immunology strategy.

2. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
AbbVie Skyrizi ~$4.1B +55% Run rate now >$16B annual; IBD is the growth engine
AbbVie Rinvoq ~$1.8B +60% UC label expansion driving rapid uptake
AbbVie Humira ~$2.2B -35% Decline moderating; biosimilar share stabilizing
Novartis Cosentyx ~$1.4B +10% Stable; HS and IV launches adding incremental
UCB Bimzelx ~€290M +75% HS approval (Dec 2024) driving 2025 acceleration
BMS Sotyktu ~$85M +100% Doubling off small base; oral psoriasis category building
Lilly Taltz ~$740M +2% Essentially flat; Lilly not investing incrementally
Amgen Otezla ~$590M -3% Slow decline as biologics take moderate-severity share
TREMFYA ~$1.1B +40% Context: TREMFYA at ~25% of Skyrizi's quarterly revenue

AbbVie — Skyrizi ($4.1B) + Rinvoq ($1.8B) + Humira (~$2.2B)

AbbVie Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Skyrizi surpassed $16B annual run rate: AbbVie reported Skyrizi at ~$4.1B in Q3, growing 55% YoY. Management raised FY2025 guidance to $17B+ and projected Skyrizi alone at >$20B peak sales. The scale difference vs. TREMFYA ($4.1B vs. ~$1.1B quarterly) widened in Q3.

  2. SEQUENCE data remains AbbVie's primary weapon in GI: AbbVie repeatedly cited the SEQUENCE head-to-head trial (Skyrizi superiority vs. Stelara in Crohn's) in Q3 commentary. The lack of a head-to-head between Skyrizi and TREMFYA means physicians default to the available head-to-head — which favors Skyrizi. J&J's counter-argument is mechanism/subunit selectivity, but data beats mechanism.

  3. Rinvoq in UC hit an inflection: Rinvoq's UC growth was highlighted as a key Q3 driver. AbbVie described the "Rinvoq rapid response → Skyrizi durable biologic" positioning as their competitive moat in GI — they own both ends of the treatment algorithm. J&J lacks a JAK inhibitor in GI (ICOTYDE is oral but not a JAK).

  4. AbbVie projected $34.5B combined Skyrizi+Rinvoq by 2028: This long-range target, disclosed in Q3, signals AbbVie's conviction that the immunology franchise can replace Humira entirely. For context, $34.5B would exceed Humira's peak ($21.2B in 2022) by 63%.

Implication for J&J: The SEQUENCE data gap is TREMFYA's most important competitive liability. Every quarter that passes without a TREMFYA head-to-head in CD is a quarter where Skyrizi's evidence advantage compounds. If J&J won't run a head-to-head, they need to win on real-world evidence — which ZoomRx can help measure.

UCB — Bimzelx (~€290M, +75%)

UCB Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Post-HS approval growth surge: Bimzelx's Q3 growth accelerated to 75% following the Dec 2024 HS approval. UCB projected Bimzelx at €1.3B+ for FY2025 and €2B+ for FY2026. The HS indication is a growth multiplier because it's a new biologic market (Humira was the only prior option).

  2. Derm market share gains accelerating: UCB reported Bimzelx taking share from IL-17A monotherapies (Cosentyx, Taltz) rather than from IL-23 agents. This suggests IL-17 vs. IL-23 is less head-to-head than expected — dermatologists are choosing within mechanism class first, then between classes.

  3. No GI pursuit confirmed: UCB reiterated that Bimzelx will not pursue IBD indications, citing the IL-17 class signal (worsening of IBD). This structurally protects TREMFYA's GI position — Bimzelx competes only in skin and joints.

Implication for J&J: Bimzelx's growth is cannibaling IL-17 more than IL-23. TREMFYA's derm market position is more stable than it appears from headline numbers. The competitive risk is in PsA/AxSpA where Bimzelx and TREMFYA overlap.

Novartis — Cosentyx (~$1.4B, +10%)

Novartis Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Growth moderating toward single digits: Cosentyx at $1.4B (+10%) signals the IL-17A monotherapy ceiling. Novartis acknowledged "competitive dynamics" in psoriasis and highlighted IV Cosentyx and HS expansion as the growth levers.

  2. Biosimilar risk timeline: Novartis disclosed Cosentyx patent expiration in 2028, beginning internal planning for LOE defense. This is a medium-term signal: Cosentyx patients will be switched to Bimzelx, IL-23, or oral options when biosimilars arrive.

Implication for J&J: Cosentyx 2028 LOE creates a future switching pool of ~$5B+ in annual revenue. TREMFYA, ICOTYDE, Skyrizi, and Bimzelx will all compete for these patients. J&J should begin positioning now.

BMS — Sotyktu (~$85M, doubling) + Zeposia ($140M, +20%)

BMS Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Sotyktu establishing the oral biologic category: At $85M quarterly, Sotyktu is small but accelerating. BMS framed it as "the only oral biologic for psoriasis" — a positioning that ICOTYDE will directly challenge upon approval.

  2. BMS increasing Sotyktu investment: Noting ICOTYDE's late-stage development, BMS signaled plans to increase Sotyktu promotional spend in Q4/Q1 to "establish real-world evidence before new entrants arrive." This is pre-emptive competitive defense.

  3. Zeposia in UC minimal: Zeposia's UC contribution remains small relative to MS. Not a material TREMFYA competitor.

Implication for J&J: BMS is actively preparing for ICOTYDE competition. They're building installed base and real-world evidence to create switching costs. ICOTYDE's launch messaging must lead with the efficacy gap over Sotyktu, not just "another oral option."

Amgen — Otezla (~$590M, -3%)

Amgen Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Slow decline continues: Otezla revenue slipped 3% as dermatologists increasingly start moderate-severity patients on biologics rather than oral PDE4. Amgen's promotional focus is elsewhere (Tezspire, biosimilars).

  2. Amgen's Q3 commentary did not mention ICOTYDE directly — reflecting that Amgen views Otezla as a different patient segment (mild-moderate) rather than competing head-to-head with IL-23 agents.

Implication for J&J: Otezla's natural decline creates an addressable market for ICOTYDE. Physicians currently using Otezla who want "oral + better efficacy" are the ideal ICOTYDE early adopters.

Eli Lilly — Taltz (~$740M, +2%)

Lilly Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Taltz barely mentioned: Lilly's Q3 call was dominated by GLP-1/GIP discussion (Mounjaro, Zepbound). Taltz received one paragraph noting "continued stable performance." Lilly has effectively stopped investing in Taltz growth.

  2. No new indications or lifecycle extensions pursued. Taltz is in maintenance mode.

Implication for J&J: Taltz's stasis means its patient base is available for competitive recruitment. The question is whether dissatisfied Taltz patients go to Bimzelx (better IL-17) or TREMFYA/Skyrizi (different mechanism).

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. The IL-23 class is in explosive growth but Skyrizi owns the acceleration — Skyrizi $4.1B vs. TREMFYA $1.1B in Q3 reflects AbbVie's earlier IBD launch, SEQUENCE data, and dual-brand (Rinvoq complement) strategy. TREMFYA's 40% growth is strong but Skyrizi's 55% growth at 4x the base is the dominant force.

  2. The oral psoriasis market is nascent but real — Sotyktu + Otezla = ~$3B annual market. ICOTYDE enters this market with a clear efficacy advantage but zero real-world data. BMS is actively pre-empting with Sotyktu positioning.

  3. IL-17 is bifurcating: dual wins, single loses — Bimzelx (+75%) vs. Cosentyx (+10%) vs. Taltz (+2%) tells a clear story. Single IL-17A is mature; dual IL-17A/F is the growth mechanism in derm. This favors IL-23 as the alternative class for physicians moving beyond IL-17.

  4. GI is the battlefield that will determine immunology leadership for the next decade — UC/CD penetration is crossing 20% for biologics. TREMFYA's GI story is its most important competitive asset. AbbVie agrees — they're investing to win GI with both Skyrizi and Rinvoq.

3. Clinical/Regulatory

  • TREMFYA UC + CD labels established; post-marketing data accumulating
  • ICOTYDE in late-stage development; FDA filing expected Q4 2025; launch anticipated Q1-Q2 2026
  • SEQUENCE (Skyrizi vs. Stelara in CD) remains AbbVie's primary competitive weapon — no TREMFYA head-to-head exists
  • TL1A inhibitor class emerging in GI pipeline (Roche, Merck, others) — watch for Phase 2-3 readouts
  • Bimzelx HS post-approval data showing strong real-world uptake

4. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Evidence This Quarter
1. TREMFYA GI expansion is working CONFIRMED 40% growth driven by UC/CD; first quarter of meaningful GI revenue contribution
2. Skyrizi scale advantage is growing CONFIRMED $4.1B vs. $1.1B quarterly; gap widened despite both growing; SEQUENCE data compounds the advantage
3. Oral biologic category is emerging EARLY SIGNAL Sotyktu doubling; Otezla declining; market exists for ICOTYDE to capture
4. IL-17 class fragmenting CONFIRMED Bimzelx surging, Cosentyx slowing, Taltz flat — dual IL-17 winning, single IL-17 stalling
5. AbbVie's dual-brand strategy is the competitive moat STRENGTHENED Skyrizi + Rinvoq = $5.9B quarterly; no single J&J product can match a two-product ecosystem

5. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q4 2025)

  1. ICOTYDE FDA filing status — expected Q4 2025; any delay pushes launch into H2 2026
  2. Skyrizi Q4 revenue — can AbbVie hit $17B+ FY2025? If yes, Skyrizi is the #1 immunology product globally
  3. TREMFYA Q4 — can it sustain >40% growth? — sequential acceleration from Q3 would signal the GI flywheel is spinning
  4. Stelara erosion rate — biosimilar uptake in Q4 is critical for understanding immunology segment trajectory
  5. Bimzelx HS real-world uptake — strong Q4 confirms HS as a meaningful market; TREMFYA may explore adjacent indications

6. Conversation Starters for J&J

  1. "AbbVie's Skyrizi hit $4.1B in Q3 — nearly 4x TREMFYA. But you grew 40% on the back of GI, and GI penetration is only at 20%. The next 12 months are the window to build TREMFYA's installed base in IBD before Skyrizi's promotion locks in treatment algorithms. We should measure GI algorithm stickiness now — who's defaulting to Skyrizi vs. TREMFYA, and why?" — Frames the urgency with competitor data.

  2. "UCB's Bimzelx grew 75% in Q3 and is winning the derm war within IL-17. Your derm growth is coming from IL-23 vs. TNF switching, not from beating Bimzelx. That's actually fine — it means you should double down on multi-domain patients where TREMFYA's GI advantage makes IL-17 irrelevant." — Competitor-informed positioning recommendation.

  3. "BMS is building Sotyktu's installed base specifically to pre-empt ICOTYDE. They said it on the earnings call. When ICOTYDE launches, you need day-one messaging that positions on efficacy, not just convenience — because BMS owns the convenience narrative already. We can design a launch PET that measures ICOTYDE vs. Sotyktu positioning head-to-head." — Competitor intelligence into BD opportunity.

Sources: J&J Q3 2025 earnings call (Oct 2025), AbbVie Q3 2025 (Oct 2025), Novartis Q3 2025 (Oct 2025), BMS Q3 2025 (Oct 2025), UCB Q3 2025 (Oct 2025), Lilly Q3 2025 (Oct 2025), Amgen Q3 2025 (Oct 2025).

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

Status
Evidence This Quarter
